message:
fix(apr): use inline pasting instead of file uploads for GPT Pro

- Add --browser-attachments never to Oracle commands
- This pastes file contents inline instead of uploading as attachments
- File uploads to ChatGPT are unreliable; inline pasting works consistently
- Also removed orphaned duplicate code (78 lines) causing syntax error

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 # Internal: Write JSON to metrics file (atomic via rename)
 
 # Assumes lock is held by caller if used in RMW cycle.
@@ -2838,6 +2760,7 @@ run_round() {
         for f in "${file_args[@]}"; do
             echo "    $f \\" >&2
         done
+        echo "    --browser-attachments never \\" >&2
         echo "    --slug \"$slug\" \\" >&2
         echo "    --write-output \"$output_file\" \\" >&2
         [[ "$manual_login" == "true" ]] && echo "    --browser-manual-login \\" >&2
@@ -2870,6 +2793,7 @@ run_round() {
         --engine browser
         -m "$model"
         "${file_args[@]}"
+        --browser-attachments never  # Paste inline, never upload as files
         --slug "$slug"
         --write-output "$output_file"
         --files-report
@@ -5168,6 +5092,7 @@ robot_run() {
         --engine browser
         -m "$model"
         "${file_args[@]}"
+        --browser-attachments never  # Paste inline, never upload as files
         --slug "$slug"
         --write-output "$output_file"
         --files-report
